text: ELECTRONIC AND MACHINE READABLE COPIES MAY BE DISTRIBUTED SO LONG AS SUCH COPIES (1) ARE FOR YOUR OR OTHERS PERSONAL USE ONLY, AND (2) ARE NOT DISTRIBUTED OR USED
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: COMMERCIALLY
text: PROHIBITED COMMERCIAL DISTRIBUTION INCLUDES BY ANY SERVICE THAT CHARGES FOR DOWNLOAD TIME OR FOR MEMBERSHIP.>>
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: A room in the house of Polonius.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Polonius and Reynaldo.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Give him this money and these notes, Reynaldo.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: I will, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You shall do marvell's wisely, good Reynaldo, Before You visit him, to make inquire Of his behaviour.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, I did intend it.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, well said, very well said. Look you, sir, Enquire me first what Danskers are in Paris; And how, and who, what means, and where they keep, What company, at what expense; and finding By this encompassment and drift of question That they do know my son, come you more nearer Than your particular demands will touch it. Take you, as 'twere, some distant knowledge of him; As thus, 'I know his father and his friends, And in part him.' Do you mark this, Reynaldo?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Ay, very well, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: 'And in part him, but,' you may say, 'not well. But if't be he I mean, he's very wild Addicted so and so'; and there put on him What forgeries you please; marry, none so rank As may dishonour him- take heed of that; But, sir, such wanton, wild, and usual slips As are companions noted and most known To youth and liberty.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: As gaming, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Ay, or drinking, fencing, swearing, quarrelling,
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: DRABBING
text: You may go so far.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, that would dishonour him.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Faith, no, as you may season it in the charge. You must not put another scandal on him, That he is open to incontinency. That's not my meaning. But breathe his faults so quaintly That they may seem the taints of liberty, The flash and outbreak of a fiery mind, A savageness in unreclaimed blood, Of general assault.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: But, my good lord-
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Wherefore should you do this?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Ay, my lord, I would know that.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, sir, here's my drift, And I believe it is a fetch of warrant. You laying these slight sullies on my son As 'twere a thing a little soil'd i' th' working, Mark you, Your party in converse, him you would sound, Having ever seen in the prenominate crimes The youth you breathe of guilty, be assur'd He closes with you in this consequence: 'Good sir,' or so, or 'friend,' or 'gentleman'- According to the phrase or the addition Of man and country-
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Very good, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And then, sir, does 'a this- 'a does- What was I about to say? By the mass, I was about to say something! Where did I leave?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: At 'closes in the consequence,' at 'friend or so,' and gentleman.'
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: At 'closes in the consequence'- Ay, marry! He closes thus: 'I know the gentleman. I saw him yesterday, or t'other day, Or then, or then, with such or such; and, as you say, There was 'a gaming; there o'ertook in's rouse; There falling out at tennis'; or perchance, 'I saw him enter such a house of sale,' Videlicet, a brothel, or so forth. See you now- Your bait of falsehood takes this carp of truth; And thus do we of wisdom and of reach, With windlasses and with assays of bias, By indirections find directions out. So, by my former lecture and advice, Shall you my son. You have me, have you not?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: My lord, I have.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: God b' wi' ye, fare ye well!
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Good my lord!                                    [Going.]
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Observe his inclination in yourself.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: I shall, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And let him ply his music.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: REYNALDO
text: Well, my lord.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Farewell!
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exit Reynaldo.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Ophelia.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: How now, Ophelia? What's the matter?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: O my lord, my lord, I have been so affrighted!
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: With what, i' th' name of God?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, as I was sewing in my closet, Lord Hamlet, with his doublet all unbrac'd, No hat upon his head, his stockings foul'd, Ungart'red, and down-gyved to his ankle; Pale as his shirt, his knees knocking each other, And with a look so piteous in purport As if he had been loosed out of hell To speak of horrors- he comes before me.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Mad for thy love?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, I do not know, But truly I do fear it.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What said he?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He took me by the wrist and held me hard; Then goes he to the length of all his arm, And, with his other hand thus o'er his brow, He falls to such perusal of my face As he would draw it. Long stay'd he so. At last, a little shaking of mine arm, And thrice his head thus waving up and down, He rais'd a sigh so piteous and profound As it did seem to shatter all his bulk And end his being. That done, he lets me go, And with his head over his shoulder turn'd He seem'd to find his way without his eyes, For out o' doors he went without their help And to the last bended their light on me.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Come, go with me. I will go seek the King. This is the very ecstasy of love, Whose violent property fordoes itself And leads the will to desperate undertakings As oft as any passion under heaven That does afflict our natures. I am sorry. What, have you given him any hard words of late?
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: No, my good lord; but, as you did command, I did repel his letters and denied His access to me.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That hath made him mad. I am sorry that with better heed and judgment I had not quoted him. I fear'd he did but trifle And meant to wrack thee; but beshrew my jealousy! By heaven, it is as proper to our age To cast beyond ourselves in our opinions As it is common for the younger sort To lack discretion. Come, go we to the King. This must be known; which, being kept close, might move More grief to hide than hate to utter love. Come.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exeunt.
scene_summary: Polonius sends Reynaldo to spy and hears Hamlet behaved strangely toward Ophelia.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: A room in the Castle.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: FLOURISH
text: [Enter King and Queen, Rosencrantz and Guildenstern, cum aliis.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Welcome, dear Rosencrantz and Guildenstern. Moreover that we much did long to see you, The need we have to use you did provoke Our hasty sending. Something have you heard Of Hamlet's transformation. So I call it, Sith nor th' exterior nor the inward man Resembles that it was. What it should be, More than his father's death, that thus hath put him So much from th' understanding of himself, I cannot dream of. I entreat you both That, being of so young days brought up with him, And since so neighbour'd to his youth and haviour, That you vouchsafe your rest here in our court Some little time; so by your companies To draw him on to pleasures, and to gather So much as from occasion you may glean, Whether aught to us unknown afflicts him thus That, open'd, lies within our remedy.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: Good gentlemen, he hath much talk'd of you, And sure I am two men there are not living To whom he more adheres. If it will please you To show us so much gentry and good will As to expend your time with us awhile For the supply and profit of our hope, Your visitation shall receive such thanks As fits a king's remembrance.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Both your Majesties Might, by the sovereign power you have of us, Put your dread pleasures more into command Than to entreaty.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: But we both obey, And here give up ourselves, in the full bent, To lay our service freely at your feet, To be commanded.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Thanks, Rosencrantz and gentle Guildenstern.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: Thanks, Guildenstern and gentle Rosencrantz. And I beseech you instantly to visit My too much changed son.- Go, some of you, And bring these gentlemen where Hamlet is.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Heavens make our presence and our practices Pleasant and helpful to him!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: Ay, amen!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exeunt Rosencrantz and Guildenstern, [with some
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Attendants].
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Polonius.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Th' ambassadors from Norway, my good lord, Are joyfully return'd.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Thou still hast been the father of good news.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Have I, my lord? Assure you, my good liege, I hold my duty as I hold my soul, Both to my God and to my gracious king; And I do think- or else this brain of mine Hunts not the trail of policy so sure As it hath us'd to do- that I have found The very cause of Hamlet's lunacy.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: O, speak of that! That do I long to hear.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Give first admittance to th' ambassadors. My news shall be the fruit to that great feast.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Thyself do grace to them, and bring them in.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exit Polonius.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: He tells me, my dear Gertrude, he hath found
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: The head and source of all your son's distemper.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: I doubt it is no other but the main, His father's death and our o'erhasty marriage.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Well, we shall sift him.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Polonius, Voltemand, and Cornelius.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Welcome, my good friends.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Say, Voltemand, what from our brother Norway?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: VOLT
text: Most fair return of greetings and desires. Upon our first, he sent out to suppress His nephew's levies; which to him appear'd To be a preparation 'gainst the Polack, But better look'd into, he truly found It was against your Highness; whereat griev'd, That so his sickness, age, and impotence Was falsely borne in hand, sends out arrests On Fortinbras; which he, in brief, obeys, Receives rebuke from Norway, and, in fine, Makes vow before his uncle never more To give th' assay of arms against your Majesty. Whereon old Norway, overcome with joy, Gives him three thousand crowns in annual fee And his commission to employ those soldiers, So levied as before, against the Polack; With an entreaty, herein further shown,
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Gives a paper.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: That it might please you to give quiet pass
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Through your dominions for this enterprise,
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: On such regards of safety and allowance
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: As therein are set down.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: It likes us well; And at our more consider'd time we'll read, Answer, and think upon this business. Meantime we thank you for your well-took labour. Go to your rest; at night we'll feast together. Most welcome home!                       Exeunt Ambassadors.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This business is well ended. My liege, and madam, to expostulate What majesty should be, what duty is, Why day is day, night is night, and time is time. Were nothing but to waste night, day, and time. Therefore, since brevity is the soul of wit, And tediousness the limbs and outward flourishes, I will be brief. Your noble son is mad. Mad call I it; for, to define true madness, What is't but to be nothing else but mad? But let that go.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: More matter, with less art.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Madam, I swear I use no art at all. That he is mad, 'tis true: 'tis true 'tis pity; And pity 'tis 'tis true. A foolish figure! But farewell it, for I will use no art. Mad let us grant him then. And now remains That we find out the cause of this effect- Or rather say, the cause of this defect, For this effect defective comes by cause. Thus it remains, and the remainder thus. Perpend. I have a daughter (have while she is mine), Who in her duty and obedience, mark, Hath given me this. Now gather, and surmise. [Reads] the letter. 'To the celestial, and my soul's idol, the most beautified Ophelia,'- That's an ill phrase, a vile phrase; 'beautified' is a vile phrase. But you shall hear. Thus:
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Reads.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: 'In her excellent white bosom, these, &c.'
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: Came this from Hamlet to her?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Good madam, stay awhile. I will be faithful.     [Reads.] 'Doubt thou the stars are fire; Doubt that the sun doth move; Doubt truth to be a liar; But never doubt I love. 'O dear Ophelia, I am ill at these numbers; I have not art to reckon my groans; but that I love thee best, O most best, believe it. Adieu. 'Thine evermore, most dear lady, whilst this machine is to him,
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: ' This, in obedience, hath my daughter shown me; And more above, hath his solicitings, As they fell out by time, by means, and place, All given to mine ear.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: But how hath she Receiv'd his love?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What do you think of me?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: As of a man faithful and honourable.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I would fain prove so. But what might you think, When I had seen this hot love on the wing (As I perceiv'd it, I must tell you that, Before my daughter told me), what might you, Or my dear Majesty your queen here, think, If I had play'd the desk or table book, Or given my heart a winking, mute and dumb, Or look'd upon this love with idle sight? What might you think? No, I went round to work And my young mistress thus I did bespeak: 'Lord Hamlet is a prince, out of thy star. This must not be.' And then I prescripts gave her, That she should lock herself from his resort, Admit no messengers, receive no tokens. Which done, she took the fruits of my advice, And he, repulsed, a short tale to make, Fell into a sadness, then into a fast, Thence to a watch, thence into a weakness, Thence to a lightness, and, by this declension, Into the madness wherein now he raves, And all we mourn for.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Do you think 'tis this?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: it may be, very like.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Hath there been such a time- I would fain know that- That I have Positively said ''Tis so,' When it prov'd otherwise.?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Not that I know.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [points to his head and shoulder] Take this from this, if this be otherwise. If circumstances lead me, I will find Where truth is hid, though it were hid indeed Within the centre.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: How may we try it further?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You know sometimes he walks for hours together Here in the lobby.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: So he does indeed.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: At such a time I'll loose my daughter to him. Be you and I behind an arras then. Mark the encounter. If he love her not, And he not from his reason fall'n thereon Let me be no assistant for a state, But keep a farm and carters.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: We will try it.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Hamlet, reading on a book.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: But look where sadly the poor wretch comes reading.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Away, I do beseech you, both away I'll board him presently. O, give me leave.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exeunt King and Queen, [with Attendants].
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: How does my good Lord Hamlet?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Well, God-a-mercy.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Do you know me, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Excellent well. You are a fishmonger.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Not I, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then I would you were so honest a man.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Honest, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, sir. To be honest, as this world goes, is to be one man pick'd out of ten thousand.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That's very true, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: For if the sun breed maggots in a dead dog, being a god kissing carrion- Have you a daughter?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I have, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let her not walk i' th' sun. Conception is a blessing, but not as your daughter may conceive. Friend, look to't.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [aside] How say you by that? Still harping on my daughter. Yet he knew me not at first. He said I was a fishmonger. He is far gone, far gone! And truly in my youth I suff'red much extremity for love- very near this. I'll speak to him again.- What do you read, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Words, words, words.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What is the matter, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Between who?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I mean, the matter that you read, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Slanders, sir; for the satirical rogue says here that old men have grey beards; that their faces are wrinkled; their eyes purging thick amber and plum-tree gum; and that they have a plentiful lack of wit, together with most weak hams. All which, sir, though I most powerfully and potently believe, yet I hold it not honesty to have it thus set down; for you yourself, sir, should be old as I am if, like a crab, you could go backward.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [aside] Though this be madness, yet there is a method in't.- Will You walk out of the air, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Into my grave?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Indeed, that is out o' th' air. [Aside] How pregnant sometimes his replies are! a happiness that often madness hits on, which reason and sanity could not so prosperously be delivered of. I will leave him and suddenly contrive the means of meeting between him and my daughter.- My honourable lord, I will most humbly take my leave of you.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: You cannot, sir, take from me anything that I will more willingly part withal- except my life, except my life, except my life,
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Enter Rosencrantz and Guildenstern.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Fare you well, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: These tedious old fools!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You go to seek the Lord Hamlet. There he is.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [to Polonius] God save you, sir!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: STAGE_DIRECTION
text: Exit [Polonius].
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: My honour'd lord!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: My most dear lord!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: My excellent good friends! How dost thou, Guildenstern? Ah, Rosencrantz! Good lads, how do ye both?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: As the indifferent children of the earth.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Happy in that we are not over-happy. On Fortune's cap we are not the very button.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nor the soles of her shoe?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Neither, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then you live about her waist, or in the middle of her favours?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Faith, her privates we.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: In the secret parts of Fortune? O! most true! she is a strumpet. What news ?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: None, my lord, but that the world's grown honest.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then is doomsday near! But your news is not true. Let me question more in particular. What have you, my good friends, deserved at the hands of Fortune that she sends you to prison hither?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Prison, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Denmark's a prison.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Then is the world one.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: A goodly one; in which there are many confines, wards, and dungeons, Denmark being one o' th' worst.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: We think not so, my lord.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, then 'tis none to you; for there is nothing either good or bad but thinking makes it so. To me it is a prison.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Why, then your ambition makes it one. 'Tis too narrow for your mind.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O God, I could be bounded in a nutshell and count myself a king of infinite space, were it not that I have bad dreams.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Which dreams indeed are ambition; for the very substance of the ambitious is merely the shadow of a dream.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: A dream itself is but a shadow.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Truly, and I hold ambition of so airy and light a quality that it is but a shadow's shadow.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then are our beggars bodies, and our monarchs and outstretch'd heroes the beggars' shadows. Shall we to th' court? for, by my fay, I cannot reason.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: BOTH
text: We'll wait upon you.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: No such matter! I will not sort you with the rest of my servants; for, to speak to you like an honest man, I am most dreadfully attended. But in the beaten way of friendship, what make you at Elsinore?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To visit you, my lord; no other occasion.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Beggar that I am, I am even poor in thanks; but I thank you; and sure, dear friends, my thanks are too dear a halfpenny. Were you not sent for? Is it your own inclining? Is it a free visitation? Come, deal justly with me. Come, come! Nay, speak.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: What should we say, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, anything- but to th' purpose. You were sent for; and there is a kind of confession in your looks, which your modesties have not craft enough to colour. I know the good King and Queen have sent for you.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To what end, my lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: That you must teach me. But let me conjure you by the rights of our fellowship, by the consonancy of our youth, by the obligation of our ever-preserved love, and by what more dear a better proposer could charge you withal, be even and direct with me, whether you were sent for or no.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [aside to Guildenstern] What say you?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [aside] Nay then, I have an eye of you.- If you love me, hold not off.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: My lord, we were sent for.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will tell you why. So shall my anticipation prevent your discovery, and your secrecy to the King and Queen moult no feather. I have of late- but wherefore I know not- lost all my mirth, forgone all custom of exercises; and indeed, it goes so heavily with my disposition that this goodly frame, the earth, seems to me a sterile promontory; this most excellent canopy, the air, look you, this brave o'erhanging firmament, this majestical roof fretted with golden fire- why, it appeareth no other thing to me than a foul and pestilent congregation of vapours. What a piece of work is a man! how noble in reason! how infinite in faculties! in form and moving how express and admirable! in action how like an angel! in apprehension how like a god! the beauty of the world, the paragon of animals! And yet to me what is this quintessence of dust? Man delights not me- no, nor woman neither, though by your smiling you seem to say so.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: My lord, there was no such stuff in my thoughts.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why did you laugh then, when I said 'Man delights not me'?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To think, my lord, if you delight not in man, what lenten entertainment the players shall receive from you. We coted them on the way, and hither are they coming to offer you service.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: He that plays the king shall be welcome- his Majesty shall have tribute of me; the adventurous knight shall use his foil and target; the lover shall not sigh gratis; the humorous man shall end his part in peace; the clown shall make those laugh whose lungs are tickle o' th' sere; and the lady shall say her mind freely, or the blank verse shall halt for't. What players are they?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Even those you were wont to take such delight in, the tragedians of the city.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How chances it they travel? Their residence, both in reputation and profit, was better both ways.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: I think their inhibition comes by the means of the late innovation.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Do they hold the same estimation they did when I was in the city? Are they so follow'd?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: No indeed are they not.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How comes it? Do they grow rusty?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Nay, their endeavour keeps in the wonted pace; but there is, sir, an eyrie of children, little eyases, that cry out on the top of question and are most tyrannically clapp'd for't. These are now the fashion, and so berattle the common stages (so they call them) that many wearing rapiers are afraid of goosequills and dare scarce come thither.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What, are they children? Who maintains 'em? How are they escoted? Will they pursue the quality no longer than they can sing? Will they not say afterwards, if they should grow themselves to common players (as it is most like, if their means are no better), their writers do them wrong to make them exclaim against their own succession.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Faith, there has been much to do on both sides; and the nation holds it no sin to tarre them to controversy. There was, for a while, no money bid for argument unless the poet and the player went to cuffs in the question.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is't possible?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: O, there has been much throwing about of brains.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Do the boys carry it away?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Ay, that they do, my lord- Hercules and his load too.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It is not very strange; for my uncle is King of Denmark, and those that would make mows at him while my father lived give twenty, forty, fifty, a hundred ducats apiece for his picture in little. 'Sblood, there is something in this more than natural, if philosophy could find it out. Flourish for the Players.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: There are the players.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Gentlemen, you are welcome to Elsinore. Your hands, come! Th' appurtenance of welcome is fashion and ceremony. Let me comply with you in this garb, lest my extent to the players (which I tell you must show fairly outwards) should more appear like entertainment than yours. You are welcome. But my uncle-father and aunt-mother are deceiv'd.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: In what, my dear lord?
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

text: I am but mad north-north-west. When the wind is southerly I know a hawk from a handsaw.

Seneca cannot be too heavy, nor Plautus too light. For the law of writ and the liberty, these are the only men.

scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I heard thee speak me a speech once, but it was never acted; or if it was, not above once; for the play, I remember, pleas'd not the million, 'twas caviary to the general; but it was (as I receiv'd it, and others, whose judgments in such matters cried in the top of mine) an excellent play, well digested in the scenes, set down with as much modesty as cunning. I remember one said there were no sallets in the lines to make the matter savoury, nor no matter in the phrase that might indict the author of affectation; but call'd it an honest method, as wholesome as sweet, and by very much more handsome than fine. One speech in't I chiefly lov'd. 'Twas AEneas' tale to Dido, and thereabout of it especially where he speaks of Priam's slaughter. If it live in your memory, begin at this line- let me see, let me see: 'The rugged Pyrrhus, like th' Hyrcanian beast-' 'Tis not so; it begins with Pyrrhus: 'The rugged Pyrrhus, he whose sable arms, Black as his purpose, did the night resemble When he lay couched in the ominous horse, Hath now this dread and black complexion smear'd With heraldry more dismal. Head to foot Now is be total gules, horridly trick'd With blood of fathers, mothers, daughters, sons, Bak'd and impasted with the parching streets, That lend a tyrannous and a damned light To their lord's murther. Roasted in wrath and fire, And thus o'ersized with coagulate gore, With eyes like carbuncles, the hellish Pyrrhus Old grandsire Priam seeks.' So, proceed you.
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Fore God, my lord, well spoken, with good accent and good discretion. 1. Play. 'Anon he finds him, Striking too short at Greeks. His antique sword, Rebellious to his arm, lies where it falls, Repugnant to command. Unequal match'd, Pyrrhus at Priam drives, in rage strikes wide; But with the whiff and wind of his fell sword Th' unnerved father falls. Then senseless Ilium, Seeming to feel this blow, with flaming top Stoops to his base, and with a hideous crash Takes prisoner Pyrrhus' ear. For lo! his sword, Which was declining on the milky head Of reverend Priam, seem'd i' th' air to stick. So, as a painted tyrant, Pyrrhus stood, And, like a neutral to his will and matter, Did nothing. But, as we often see, against some storm, A silence in the heavens, the rack stand still, The bold winds speechless, and the orb below As hush as death- anon the dreadful thunder Doth rend the region; so, after Pyrrhus' pause, Aroused vengeance sets him new awork; And never did the Cyclops' hammers fall On Mars's armour, forg'd for proof eterne, With less remorse than Pyrrhus' bleeding sword Now falls on Priam. Out, out, thou strumpet Fortune! All you gods, In general synod take away her power; Break all the spokes and fellies from her wheel, And bowl the round nave down the hill of heaven, As low as to the fiends!
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS

text: It shall to the barber's, with your beard.- Prithee say on. He's for a jig or a tale of bawdry, or he sleeps. Say on; come to Hecuba. 1. Play. 'But who, O who, had seen the mobled queen-'
scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET

scene_summary: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern arrive; Hamlet plans to use players to test Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That's good! 'Mobled queen' is good. 1. Play. 'Run barefoot up and down, threat'ning the flames With bisson rheum; a clout upon that head Where late the diadem stood, and for a robe, About her lank and all o'erteemed loins, A blanket, in the alarm of fear caught up- Who this had seen, with tongue in venom steep'd 'Gainst Fortune's state would treason have pronounc'd. But if the gods themselves did see her then, When she saw Pyrrhus make malicious sport In Mincing with his sword her husband's limbs, The instant burst of clamour that she made (Unless things mortal move them not at all) Would have made milch the burning eyes of heaven And passion in the gods.'

speaker: HAMLET
text: 'Tis well. I'll have thee speak out the rest of this soon.- Good my lord, will you see the players well bestow'd? Do you hear? Let them be well us'd; for they are the abstract and brief chronicles of the time. After your death you were better have a bad epitaph than their ill report while you live.

speaker: HAMLET
text: To be, or not to be- that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune Or to take arms against a sea of troubles, And by opposing end them. To die- to sleep- No more; and by a sleep to say we end The heartache, and the thousand natural shocks That flesh is heir to. 'Tis a consummation Devoutly to be wish'd. To die- to sleep. To sleep- perchance to dream: ay, there's the rub! For in that sleep of death what dreams may come When we have shuffled off this mortal coil, Must give us pause. There's the respect That makes calamity of so long life. For who would bear the whips and scorns of time, Th' oppressor's wrong, the proud man's contumely, The pangs of despis'd love, the law's delay, The insolence of office, and the spurns That patient merit of th' unworthy takes, When he himself might his quietus make With a bare bodkin? Who would these fardels bear, To grunt and sweat under a weary life, But that the dread of something after death- The undiscover'd country, from whose bourn No traveller returns- puzzles the will, And makes us rather bear those ills we have Than fly to others that we know not of? Thus conscience does make cowards of us all, And thus the native hue of resolution Is sicklied o'er with the pale cast of thought, And enterprises of great pith and moment With this regard their currents turn awry And lose the name of action.- Soft you now! The fair Ophelia!- Nymph, in thy orisons Be all my sins rememb'red.
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Good my lord, How does your honour for this many a day?
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: HAMLET
text: I humbly thank you; well, well, well.
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My honour'd lord, you know right well you did, And with them words of so sweet breath compos'd As made the things more rich. Their perfume lost, Take these again; for to the noble mind Rich gifts wax poor when givers prove unkind. There, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ha, ha! Are you honest?

text: My lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, truly; for the power of beauty will sooner transform honesty from what it is to a bawd than the force of honesty can translate beauty into his likeness. This was sometime a paradox, but now the time gives it proof. I did love you once.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Get thee to a nunnery! Why wouldst thou be a breeder of sinners? I am myself indifferent honest, but yet I could accuse me of such things that it were better my mother had not borne me. I am very proud, revengeful, ambitious; with more offences at my beck than I have thoughts to put them in, imagination to give them shape, or time to act them in. What should such fellows as I do, crawling between earth and heaven? We are arrant knaves all; believe none of us. Go thy ways to a nunnery. Where's your father?

speaker: HAMLET
text: If thou dost marry, I'll give thee this plague for thy dowry: be thou as chaste as ice, as pure as snow, thou shalt not escape calumny. Get thee to a nunnery. Go, farewell. Or if thou wilt needs marry, marry a fool; for wise men know well enough what monsters you make of them. To a nunnery, go; and quickly too. Farewell.
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: OPHELIA

scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: HAMLET
text: I have heard of your paintings too, well enough. God hath given you one face, and you make yourselves another. You jig, you amble, and you lisp; you nickname God's creatures and make your wantonness your ignorance. Go to, I'll no more on't! it hath made me mad. I say, we will have no more marriages. Those that are married already- all but one- shall live; the rest shall keep as they are. To a nunnery, go.                            Exit.
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: OPHELIA
text: O, what a noble mind is here o'erthrown! The courtier's, scholar's, soldier's, eye, tongue, sword, Th' expectancy and rose of the fair state, The glass of fashion and the mould of form, Th' observ'd of all observers- quite, quite down! And I, of ladies most deject and wretched, That suck'd the honey of his music vows, Now see that noble and most sovereign reason, Like sweet bells jangled, out of tune and harsh; That unmatch'd form and feature of blown youth Blasted with ecstasy. O, woe is me T' have seen what I have seen, see what I see!
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Love? his affections do not that way tend; Nor what he spake, though it lack'd form a little, Was not like madness. There's something in his soul O'er which his melancholy sits on brood; And I do doubt the hatch and the disclose Will be some danger; which for to prevent, I have in quick determination Thus set it down: he shall with speed to England For the demand of our neglected tribute. Haply the seas, and countries different, With variable objects, shall expel This something-settled matter in his heart, Whereon his brains still beating puts him thus From fashion of himself. What think you on't?
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: POLONIUS
text: It shall do well. But yet do I believe The origin and commencement of his grief Sprung from neglected love.- How now, Ophelia? You need not tell us what Lord Hamlet said. We heard it all.- My lord, do as you please; But if you hold it fit, after the play Let his queen mother all alone entreat him To show his grief. Let her be round with him; And I'll be plac'd so please you, in the ear Of all their conference. If she find him not, To England send him; or confine him where Your wisdom best shall think.
scene_summary: Hamlet reflects on existence, rejects Ophelia, and Claudius suspects danger.
keywords: to be or not to be

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: It shall be so. Madness in great ones must not unwatch'd go.         Exeunt.
